name: Chai
description: A mix of black tea and spices like cinnamon, cardamom, ginger, black pepper, and star anise. Inspired by Indian masala chai.
flavorNotes: Cinnamon, cardamom, ginger, black pepper kick
tips: Tastes best as a latte with milk and honey. You can also add some Turmeric Ginger (available as a House Add-on) for more warmth.

name: Earl Grey
description: A decaffeinated version of the classic Earl Grey. It has the standard black tea base with oil of bergamot for a citrus flavor.
flavorNotes: Bergamot citrus, smooth black tea, clean finish
tips: A good choice for the evening since it has no caffeine. Nice with a splash of milk.

name: Green Ginger
description: A green tea blend with ginger, lemongrass, and notes of pear. It has a slightly spicy and fruity taste.
tips: The pear flavor is quite noticeable. It is best to drink it without milk.

name: Passion
description: A caffeine-free infusion with hibiscus, orange peel, rosehips, and a bit of cinnamon. It has a tart and tropical profile.
tips: Works well as an iced tea. The tartness goes well with a spoonful of Greek Honey (available as a House Add-on).

name: Zen
description: A blend of green tea, spearmint, lemon verbena, and lemongrass. It is a refreshing cup that balances light mint with citrus notes.
flavorNotes: Spearmint, lemon verbena, lemongrass, green tea
tips: Best to drink this one as is, since the mint and citrus are already balanced.

name: After Meals
description: A Moroccan digestive blend that uses caraway, rosemary, and peppermint. It is made to be used after meals.
tips: Drink after a heavy dinner to help with digestion. Best without any added sugar or honey.

name: Royal Earl Grey
description: A variation of Earl Grey with blueberry notes added to the bergamot flavor, using a Chinese black tea base.
tips: Adding a few Rose Petals (available as a House Add-on) can give it a nice floral touch.

name: Sheherazade
description: A blend of black tea and green tea with mango. Named after the character from One Thousand and One Nights.
flavorNotes: Mango, mixed black and green tea
tips: The mix of different teas gives it quite a bit of depth. Best to drink it as it is.

name: Yunnan Red Tea
description: A black tea from the Yunnan province of China. It has a malty taste and a smooth finish.
tips: A good tea for the morning. It can take a bit of milk if you prefer.

name: La Belle Humeur
description: An organic herbal blend with raspberry leaves, lemongrass, marjoram, and wild rose. It has a light and fresh taste.
flavorNotes: Raspberry leaf, lemongrass, marjoram, wild rose
tips: A good afternoon tea. You can add some Bee Balm (available as a House Add-on) for more floral notes.

name: Sublime Chamomile
description: An organic chamomile from Camellia Sinensis mixed with rose and lemongrass. It is a floral take on the traditional bedtime tea.
tips: Good for the evening. A bit of Greek Honey (available as a House Add-on) makes it very soothing.

name: Jin Shuan
description: A Taiwanese Oolong known as Milk Oolong. It has a naturally buttery and creamy texture without any added flavors.
tips: Best to drink this alone to really taste the smooth texture.

name: Earl Grey de Marie-Antoinette
description: An organic blend of jasmine green tea and black tea with rose petals and bergamot. It is a floral version of Earl Grey.
tips: Drink it black to avoid covering up the jasmine and rose flavors.

name: New Monk's Blend
description: A mix of black and green tea from Sri Lanka with vanilla and jasmine flavors.
tips: The vanilla adds a natural sweetness, so you might not need any sugar.

name: Peppermint
description: A pure peppermint herbal tea. It has a very refreshing and cool taste from the natural menthol.
tips: A good choice for settling the stomach. You can steep it for a long time to get a stronger mint taste.

name: Feng Yun Jasmine
description: A Chinese green tea that has been scented with jasmine flowers. It has a very strong floral perfume.
flavorNotes: Sweet jasmine, vegetal green tea notes
tips: Keep the steeping time short for this one. You can use the same leaves for 3 or 4 infusions.

name: Le Secret du Bonheur
description: A refreshing herbal blend made with fennel seeds, mint, and hibiscus. It is meant to help with digestion.
tips: Nice to have after a meal. The fennel adds a natural sweetness.

name: Mimosa Framboise Biologique
description: An organic green tea blend with raspberry, rose, and strawberry notes. It has a fruity and floral taste.
tips: Makes a good iced tea for the summer, where the berry flavors come out more.

name: Rose Petals
description: Dried rose petals used for adding a floral scent and flavor to other teas.
tips: You can add these to black teas or chamomile for a bit of flower flavor.

name: Egyptian Mint
description: A type of mint from Egypt that is strong and earthier than regular peppermint.
tips: Can be added to black teas like Chai or Assam if you want a minty flavor.

name: Turmeric Ginger
description: A mix of turmeric and ginger in powder form. It can be used for making golden milk or added to other teas.
tips: You can mix this into Chai or Assam with milk. A tiny bit of black pepper helps with the absorption of the turmeric.

name: Bee Balm
description: Dried bee balm flowers that have a spicy and floral taste, somewhat like oregano.
tips: Nice to add to herbal blends like La belle humeur for more flavor.

name: Dragon Pearls
description: Green tea from Fujian that has been rolled into small pearls and scented with jasmine flowers.
tips: You can watch the pearls open as they steep. They can be infused several times and usually get sweeter.

name: Thé en Fleur
description: A hand-tied bundle of green tea leaves and flowers that opens up into a flower shape when steeped.
flavorNotes: Light green tea, subtle floral notes
tips: Use a clear glass teapot so you can see the flower bloom.

name: Green Tea with Coconut
description: A green tea with coconut, lemongrass, ginger, and vanilla flavors.
flavorNotes: Coconut, lemongrass, ginger, hint of vanilla
tips: This is also sold as 'Bangkok' by Harney & Sons. It works well both hot and iced.

name: Immune +++
description: A herbal blend with lemongrass, lemon verbena, ginger, and zinc to help support the immune system.
flavorNotes: Lemon verbena, ginger, hint of licorice, +zinc+
tips: The zinc content means it is best to limit yourself to a couple of cups a day. A spoonful of Greek Honey (available as a House Add-on) can make it more soothing. Warning: Do not mix with multi-vitamins or zinc supplements.

name: Chamomile
description: Pure chamomile flowers sourced from Egypt. A very simple and relaxing herbal tea.
tips: A classic tea for before bed. It goes very well with a bit of Greek Honey (available as a House Add-on).

name: Dragon Well/Long Jing
description: A well-known Chinese green tea from Zhejiang. The leaves are flat and it has a nutty taste.
tips: Use water that is not too hot to avoid a bitter taste. Best enjoyed on its own.

name: Organic Lapsang Souchong
description: A black tea from China that is dried over pinewood fires, giving it a very smoky flavor.
tips: It has a very strong taste that isn't for everyone. Be careful not to steep it for too long.

name: Ruby 18
description: A high-quality black tea from Taiwan grown near Sun Moon Lake. It has a very complex set of flavors.
flavorNotes: Cinnamon, mint, honey, hint of cocoa
tips: A special tea that should be drunk pure to taste the cinnamon and mint notes.

name: Rosehips
description: Dried rosehip fruit that is naturally high in Vitamin C. It adds a tart flavor and a red color to herbal blends.
tips: You can add this to other herbal teas for a boost of Vitamin C. It goes well with hibiscus teas.

name: SOS Immunity +++
description: An organic herbal blend with ginger, apple, orange peel, hibiscus, echinacea, turmeric, and cinnamon. It is designed to help support the body when feeling unwell.
tips: A good tea for when you have a cold. Adding a bit of Greek Honey (available as a House Add-on) can help.

name: Greek Mountain Tea
description: Traditional Greek mountain tea made from the Sideritis plant. It is an earthy herbal tea that is very popular in Greece.
tips: The traditional way to drink this is with local Greek Honey (available as a House Add-on) and a squeeze of lemon.

name: Lemon & Ginger Probiotics +++
description: A blend of ginger, lemon, and turmeric that also includes probiotics. It is intended for digestive health.
tips: A good option for when you aren't feeling well. It goes well with Greek Honey (available as a House Add-on).

name: Lemon Ginger
description: A caffeine-free blend with ginger root, lemongrass, and hibiscus. It has a warming and slightly tart flavor.
tips: You can steep it for longer if you want a stronger ginger taste.

name: Darjeeling
description: A black tea from the Darjeeling region in India. It is lighter than most other black teas and has a distinctive grape-like flavor.
tips: Best to drink without milk to taste the delicate grape notes.

name: Hot Cinnamon Spice
description: A popular black tea blend with three types of cinnamon, orange peel, and sweet cloves. It has a very sweet taste despite having no added sugar.
tips: It is naturally sweet, so it doesn't need any extra sugar. Good as a dessert tea.

name: Cherry Blossom
description: A green tea with cherry blossom and vanilla flavors. It is inspired by the Japanese spring season.
flavorNotes: Cherry blossom, vanilla, light green tea
tips: A light tea that should not be steeped for too long. Best enjoyed by itself.

name: Classic Green Tea
description: A simple green tea.
tips: Can be used as a base for lattes. You can also add some Egyptian Mint (available as a House Add-on) for a different taste.

name: Raspberry Rooibos
description: A rooibos tea from South Africa with raspberry flavor. It is naturally caffeine-free and has an earthy and fruity taste.
tips: Tastes good both hot and cold. It has enough sweetness on its own.

name: Ayurved'ahhh
description: A rooibos blend with mint, wild rose, and rosehip. It is inspired by Ayurvedic traditions and is caffeine-free.
flavorNotes: Earthy rooibos, wild rose, rosehip, mint
tips: A good tea for relaxing in the evening. Steep for the full 7 minutes for a better taste.

name: Earl Grey Marnier
description: A black tea blend that combines the citrus flavor of bergamot with orange and tangerine notes. It is a fruity taking on Earl Grey.
tips: It has a very bright citrus scent. You can try it with a splash of milk for a creamy citrus taste.

name: Feux d'artifices
description: A black tea base from Sri Lanka blended with raspberry and jasmine. It has a fruity and floral character.
tips: The raspberry flavor is quite distinct. Best to drink it as it is to enjoy the balance of fruit and flower.

name: Mango Sencha
description: A Japanese Sencha green tea flavoured with mango and mixed with pineapple pieces. It is a refreshing and tropical blend.
tips: This tea makes a very good iced tea. Be sure the water is not boiling so the green tea does not get bitter.

name: Perles du Saguenay
description: A black tea from the Nuwara Eliya region of Sri Lanka mixed with blueberry. It is named after the blueberries of the Saguenay region in Quebec.
tips: The blueberry taste is gentle. It is best enjoyed without adding milk.

name: Camomile Lavande
description: A herbal tea that combines chamomile and lavender. It is a relaxing blend for the evening.
tips: A good tea for before bed. You can add a spoonful of Greek Honey (available as a House Add-on) for a more soothing cup.

name: Jin Long Mo Li
description: A Chinese green tea from the Jiangxi province that has been scented with jasmine flowers. It has a light and floral taste.
flavorNotes: Jasmine, light green tea, subtle bitterness
tips: You can brew the same leaves multiple times. It gets more delicate with each infusion.

name: Citron Myrtille
description: A Japanese Sencha green tea blend with lemongrass, ginger, and blueberry leaf.
tips: Keep the water at 75°C to avoid the tea becoming too bitter.

name: Citronelle Gingembre
description: A simple herbal infusion made with lemongrass and ginger. It is caffeine-free and has a clean taste.
tips: The longer you steep it, the more the ginger flavor will come out.

name: Pêche Zen
description: A rooibos tea blend with peach pieces, calendula, and blackberry leaf. It is naturally caffeine-free.
tips: It is a very good tea to have cold. You can brew it strong and then add ice.

name: Citron Punché (Biologique)
description: An organic herbal blend that has a strong lemon flavor with a bit of ginger. It is designed to be very refreshing when served cold.
tips: One of the best options for making iced tea. Brew it strong and serve with plenty of ice.

name: Me Cha Tamame
description: A high-quality Japanese green tea made from young buds and leaves. It has a rich, full-bodied taste with marine notes.
flavorNotes: Marine umami, rich texture, grassy sweetness
tips: A very well-balanced tea. Use water at 75°C to get the best flavor from the buds.

name: Nuit de Rêves
description: An evening herbal blend with hibiscus, fennel, and rosemary. It is designed to help with relaxation and digestion.
flavorNotes: Tart hibiscus, sweet fennel, aromatic rosemary
tips: You can steep it for up to 10 minutes for more digestive benefits. A bit of Greek Honey (available as a House Add-on) goes well with the fennel and rosemary.

name: Congou à la Rose
description: A Chinese black tea from the Fujian province that is mixed with rose petals. It is a smooth and aromatic tea.
tips: You can add extra Rose Petals (available as a House Add-on) if you want a stronger floral taste.

name: Assam
description: A strong, malty black tea from the Assam region of India. It has a full-bodied taste and a dark amber color.
tips: A good tea to have with milk. It also goes well with some Egyptian Mint (available as a House Add-on) for a refreshing change.

name: Cream of Earl Grey
description: An organic black tea with bergamot flavor and vanilla. It also contains marigold and cornflower petals.
tips: Many people like this one as a latte with frothed milk. The vanilla flavor is usually enough that you don't need sugar.

name: Greek Honey
description: Pure honey from Greece. It has a rich and floral taste that is more complex than usual clover honey.
tips: Add a small amount to chamomile or mountain tea. It is a House Add-on that you can ask for.
